result.stdout:
Description:
  VertiPaq-Analyzer CLI

Usage:
  vpax [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  browse   (Experimental) Browse metadata of a tabular model in a VPAX package file
  export   Export a VPAX file from a tabular model
  package  (Experimental) Manage a VPAX package file

commands.payload:
Description:
  VertiPaq-Analyzer CLI

Usage:
  vpax [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  browse   (Experimental) Browse metadata of a tabular model in a VPAX package file
  export   Export a VPAX file from a tabular model
  package  (Experimental) Manage a VPAX package file

result.stdout:
Description:
  (Experimental) Browse metadata of a tabular model in a VPAX package file

Usage:
  vpax browse [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  m, model         Display tabular model information
  t, table         Display table information
  c, column        Display column information
  r, relationship  Display relationship information

commands.payload:
Description:
  (Experimental) Browse metadata of a tabular model in a VPAX package file

Usage:
  vpax browse [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  m, model         Display tabular model information
  t, table         Display table information
  c, column        Display column information
  r, relationship  Display relationship information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Display column information

Usage:
  vpax browse column [options]

Options:
  --vpax <path> (REQUIRED)  Path to the VPAX file
  --table <name>            Specify the table name
  --exclude-hidden          Specify whether to exclude hidden objects
  --order-by <number>       Specify the column number to order by
  --top <number>            Specify the maximum number of objects to display
  -?, -h, --help            Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Export a VPAX file from a tabular model

Usage:
  vpax export <path> <connection-string> [options]

Arguments:
  <path>               Path to write the VPAX file
  <connection-string>  Connection string for the tabular model e.g. Provider=MSOLAP;Data Source=<SERVER>;Initial Catalog=<DATABASE>

Options:
  --column-batch-size <column-batch-size>            Number of rows processed at a time during column statistics analysis [default: 50]
  --direct-query-mode <Full|None>                    Direct Query extraction mode [default: Full]
  --direct-lake-mode <Full|Referenced|ResidentOnly>  Direct Lake extraction mode [default: Full]
  --exclude-bim                                      Exclude the BIM model (Model.bim) from the export [default: False]
  --exclude-vpa                                      Exclude the VPA model (DaxVpaView.json) from the export [default: False]
  --overwrite                                        Overwrite the VPAX file if it already exists [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Export a VPAX file from a tabular model

Usage:
  vpax export <path> <connection-string> [options]

Arguments:
  <path>               Path to write the VPAX file
  <connection-string>  Connection string for the tabular model e.g. Provider=MSOLAP;Data Source=<SERVER>;Initial Catalog=<DATABASE>

Options:
  --column-batch-size <column-batch-size>            Number of rows processed at a time during column statistics analysis [default: 50]
  --direct-query-mode <Full|None>                    Direct Query extraction mode [default: Full]
  --direct-lake-mode <Full|Referenced|ResidentOnly>  Direct Lake extraction mode [default: Full]
  --exclude-bim                                      Exclude the BIM model (Model.bim) from the export [default: False]
  --exclude-vpa                                      Exclude the VPA model (DaxVpaView.json) from the export [default: False]
  --overwrite                                        Overwrite the VPAX file if it already exists [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  (Experimental) Manage a VPAX package file

Usage:
  vpax package [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  extract  Extract all files from a VPAX package
  show     Show details of a VPAX package file

commands.payload:
Description:
  (Experimental) Manage a VPAX package file

Usage:
  vpax package [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  extract  Extract all files from a VPAX package
  show     Show details of a VPAX package file

result.stdout:
Description:
  Extract all files from a VPAX package

Usage:
  vpax package extract [options]

Options:
  --vpax <vpax> (REQUIRED)  Path to the VPAX file
  --output <output>         Path for the output directory. If not specified, the VPAX directory will be used
  --overwrite               Overwrite the extracted file if it already exists [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help            Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Extract all files from a VPAX package

Usage:
  vpax package extract [options]

Options:
  --vpax <vpax> (REQUIRED)  Path to the VPAX file
  --output <output>         Path for the output directory. If not specified, the VPAX directory will be used
  --overwrite               Overwrite the extracted file if it already exists [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help            Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Show details of a VPAX package file

Usage:
  vpax package show [options]

Options:
  --vpax <vpax> (REQUIRED)  Path to the VPAX file
  -?, -h, --help            Show help and usage information
